data: If the note mentions infected hepatic fluid collection, prefer J81 (abscess of liver) over K86 (choledocholithiasis).
Mention of infected hepatic fluid collection without choledocholithiasis cues.
If the note mentions hypoxemia (e.g., O2 saturation <95% on room air), prefer J98 (hypoxemia) over I50 (heart failure).
Mention of hypoxemia (O2 91% RA) without heart failure cues.
Avoid Z80 (family history of malignant neoplasm) unless the note explicitly states a first-degree relative's malignancy.
Mention of family history of prostate cancer in a first-degree relative.
If the note mentions tachycardia-induced cardiomyopathy, prefer I42 over I48.
If hypertension is documented alongside heart failure or cardiomyopathy, prefer I13 over I10.
Hypertension with heart failure misclassified as essential hypertension.
If the note explicitly states multiple myeloma, use C90 instead of C88.
Multiple myeloma misclassified as unspecified neoplasm.
If the note specifies GI bleed due to AVMs, use K22 instead of K25.
AVM-related GI bleed misclassified as gastric ulcer.
If anemia is documented alongside multiple myeloma, use D62 instead of D64.
Anemia in neoplastic disease misclassified as unspecified anemia.
If the note mentions a Watchman procedure, avoid T88 as it is a left atrial appendage closure device, not a valve complication.
Watchman procedure misclassified as valve complication.
If the note mentions dark black stool and GI bleed due to AVMs, include K92 for gastrointestinal hemorrhage.
Gastrointestinal hemorrhage due to AVMs omitted.
If the note explicitly states multiple myeloma stage III, include C90.
If hypertension is documented alongside cardiomyopathy, include I13 for hypertensive heart disease.
If the note mentions tachycardia-induced cardiomyopathy, include I42.
If anemia is documented alongside multiple myeloma, include D62.
If the note mentions a sister with breast cancer, include Z87 for family history of malignant neoplasm.
Family history of malignant neoplasm omitted.
If the note mentions chronic SP tube use, include Z99.83 for dependence on urinary catheter.
Mention of chronic suprapubic catheter use without explicit device dependence code.
When diabetes is documented with insulin use, prefer E11.9 over unspecified codes.
Diabetes mellitus with insulin use but unspecified code.
When neurogenic bladder is explicitly mentioned, assign N31.4 (Neurogenic bladder).
Neurogenic bladder documented without specific code.
Documented recurrent UTIs and pyelonephritis should map to N39.0 (Recurrent urinary tract infection).
Recurrent UTIs and pyelonephritis without specific infection code.
Bilateral lower extremity paralysis from spinal injury requires G82.2 (Paraplegia).
Paraplegia from spinal cord infarction without mobility code.
When chronic constipation is documented, assign K56.0 (Chronic constipation).
Spinal cord infarction and frontal lobe strokes require I69.39 (Late effects of cerebrovascular disease).
Cerebrovascular disease with late effects not coded.
Chronic abdominal pain should be coded as R18.9 (Abdominal pain, unspecified).
Abdominal pain without appropriate symptom code.
Seropositive rheumatoid arthritis requires M05.9 (Rheumatoid arthritis, unspecified).
Seropositive RA without rheumatoid condition code.
Multiple bowel resections and colectomy require Z98.89 (Other postprocedural states).
History of multiple bowel surgeries without postoperative code.
If the note mentions a benign abdominal wall mass, prefer N80 over the unspecified D27.
Mention of abdominal wall mass without specifying the correct code.
If the note mentions seasonal allergies, prefer J30 over the unspecified J84.
Mention of seasonal allergies without specifying allergic rhinitis.
If the note mentions a Pancoast tumor, prefer C34.1 (Malignant neoplasm of upper lobe, lung) over unspecified C34.9.
Mention of Pancoast tumor without explicit neuroendocrine carcinoma cues.
If the note mentions a lung mass and effusion, prefer J96.0 (Malignant pleural effusion) over unspecified J96.9.
Mention of lung mass and effusion without explicit malignancy confirmation.
If the note mentions possible CHF and diastolic dysfunction, prefer I50.2 (Diastolic heart failure) over unspecified I50.9.
Mention of possible CHF without explicit diastolic heart failure cues.
If the note mentions warfarin use, include E87.0 (Disorder of coagulation due to anticoagulant use).
Mention of anticoagulant use without explicit coagulation disorder coding.
If the note lists depression in PMH, include F32.9 (Major depressive disorder, unspecified).
Mention of depression in medical history without explicit depressive disorder coding.
If the note mentions hypertension without heart disease, prefer I10 (Essential hypertension) over I12 (Hypertensive heart disease).
Mention of hypertension without explicit heart disease association.
If the note mentions gout in PMH, include M10.9 (Gout, unspecified).
Mention of gout without explicit gout coding.
If the note mentions hypoxia and dyspnea requiring supplemental oxygen, include Z95.898 (Dependence on oxygen therapy).
Mention of hypoxia and dyspnea without explicit oxygen dependence coding.
If the note mentions family history of pancreatic cancer, include Z87.0 (Family history of malignant neoplasm of digestive system).
Mention of family history of pancreatic cancer without explicit digestive system malignancy coding.
Avoid including S57 unless the admission note explicitly mentions injury to the forearm.
Crush injury to forearm without explicit mention in the admission note.
Avoid including T79 unless the admission note explicitly mentions compartment syndrome.
Compartment syndrome of hand without explicit mention in the admission note.
If the note mentions HCV, include B18 for Hepatitis C, unspecified.
Mention of HCV without explicit ICD-10 code for hepatitis C.
Avoid coding K65 (abdominal abscess) if only spinal epidural abscess is documented.
Mention of epidural abscess without abdominal abscess cues.
Do not code T82 (postoperative wound infection) if no surgical procedure is documented in the admission note.
Postoperative wound infection code without surgical procedure mention.
Avoid coding N13 (pyelonephritis) if only hydronephrosis is mentioned without infection.
Pyelonephritis code without explicit infection cues.
Do not code G83 (chronic pain syndrome) if only acute postoperative pain is documented.
Chronic pain code without chronic pain documentation.
Avoid coding L76 (herpes zoster) if no skin lesions or rash are described.
Herpes zoster code without skin lesion documentation.
If opioid use disorder is documented, prefer F11 over Y83/Y84 for adverse drug effects.
Opioid dependence code (F11) vs unspecified adverse drug effect (Y83/Y84).
If the patient leaves AMA, use Z91.89 instead of Z59 for encounter classification.
General encounter code (Z59) vs AMA discharge code (Z91.89).
For opioid use disorder, use F11.20 (opioid dependence) instead of F17 for unspecified mental/behavioral disorders.
Avoid Z00 for specific inpatient encounters; use Z91.89 for AMA discharges.
General adult exam code (Z00) vs specific encounter.
When insulin pump therapy is explicitly mentioned in the admission note, include Z96 (Dependence on insulin) and Z79 (Long term use of insulin) as comorbidities.
Insulin pump use documented without Z96/Z79 coding for insulin dependence.
Avoid R10 (Abdominal pain) when N10 (Acute pyelonephritis) is explicitly documented in the admission note's physical exam (left-sided CVA tenderness).
Abdominal pain code R10 used alongside more specific pyelonephritis code N10.
Avoid including B18 unless the note explicitly states a current Hepatitis C diagnosis or active management.
Hepatitis C (B18) included without current diagnosis cues.
Do not assign Z98 unless anticoagulant therapy is explicitly mentioned in the admission note.
Long-term anticoagulant use (Z98) included without medication documentation.
For active bipolar disorder, prefer F31 over Z83 unless the note explicitly states a resolved or historical diagnosis.
Personal history of mental disorder (Z83) included for active bipolar disorder (F31).
Avoid Z85 unless the note mentions a resolved chronic condition not already coded as active.
Personal history of other diseases (Z85) included without specific chronic condition cues.
Do not assign R20 for stable neurological deficits from prior trauma unless new abnormalities are documented.
Abnormal neurological findings (R20) included for stable TBI sequelae.
Include Z87 when traumatic brain injury is explicitly listed in past medical history.
Traumatic brain injury personal history (Z87) omitted despite PMH documentation.
Assign R33 when medications like tamsulosin are documented for urinary retention.
Urinary retention (R33) omitted despite tamsulosin prescription.
Include Z59 when the note states the patient cannot live independently due to medical conditions.
Homelessness (Z59) omitted despite inability to live independently.
Assign N40 when tamsulosin is prescribed for urinary symptoms consistent with BPH.
Benign prostatic hyperplasia (N40) omitted despite tamsulosin use.
If the note mentions head/neck SCC with a tonsil location, prefer C09 over C00.
Mention of head/neck squamous cell carcinoma without specifying tonsil location.
If the note mentions multiple myeloma, prefer C90 over C88.
Mention of multiple myeloma without using the specific plasma cell neoplasm code.
If anemia is mentioned without malignancy context, prefer D64 over D63.
Anemia coded as neoplastic-related without explicit context of malignancy.
If the note mentions chronic diarrhea, prefer R15 over R10.
Abdominal pain coded without specifying diarrhea as the underlying issue.
If the note mentions incontinence without infection, prefer R32 over N39.
Urinary incontinence coded as infection without retention context.
If the note mentions chronic diarrhea, prefer R15 over K62.
Diarrhea coded as intestinal disease without specifying chronicity.
If the note mentions failure to thrive and PEG tube, prefer E46 over R63.
Malnutrition coded as unspecified without severity context.
If the note mentions family history of multiple cancers, include Z80, Z85, and Z87.
Failure to code family history of multiple cancers beyond the initial Z80.
If the note mentions TSH 19, include E03.
Failure to code hypothyroidism despite elevated TSH in admission labs.
If the note mentions hyponatremia and hypochloremia, include E87.
Failure to code electrolyte imbalances despite hyponatremia and hypochloremia.
If the note mentions palliative nivolumab, include Z51.
Failure to code antineoplastic chemotherapy despite nivolumab administration.
If the note mentions clonazepam and lorazepam, include F41.
Failure to code anxiety disorder despite clonazepam and lorazepam prescriptions.
If the note mentions venlafaxine, include F32.
Failure to code major depressive disorder despite venlafaxine prescriptions.
If the note mentions sumatriptan, include G43.
Failure to code migraine despite sumatriptan prescriptions.
If the note lists multiple chronic medications, include Z90 and Z93.
Failure to code long-term medication use despite multiple chronic prescriptions.
If the note explicitly mentions vascular dementia, ensure F01 is included in the diagnosis codes.
Failure to code vascular dementia when explicitly mentioned in past medical history.
If the note explicitly mentions cerebral amyloid angiopathy, ensure I68 is included in the diagnosis codes.
Failure to code cerebral amyloid angiopathy when explicitly mentioned in present illness.
When altered mental status is documented in a patient with dementia, prefer G93 (Encephalopathy) over F05 (Delirium) if no acute intoxication or metabolic derangement is explicitly stated.
Confusing encephalopathy with unspecified delirium in dementia context.
If the note explicitly mentions hypertensive heart disease, ensure I11 is included in the diagnosis codes.
Failure to code hypertensive heart disease when explicitly listed in past medical history.
If the note explicitly mentions hyperlipidemia, ensure E78 is included in the diagnosis codes.
Failure to code hyperlipidemia when explicitly listed in past medical history.
If the note explicitly mentions hypothyroidism, ensure E03 is included in the diagnosis codes.
Failure to code hypothyroidism when explicitly listed in past medical history.
If the note explicitly mentions osteoporosis, ensure M81 is included in the diagnosis codes.
Failure to code osteoporosis when explicitly listed in past medical history.
If the note explicitly mentions use of stool softeners (e.g., docusate sodium), ensure K59 is included in the diagnosis codes.
Failure to code constipation when stool softener use is explicitly documented.
When cerebral amyloid angiopathy is explicitly mentioned with prior infarcts, prefer I68 (Cerebral amyloid angiopathy) over I63 (Cerebral infarction) for chronic vascular pathology.
Confusing cerebral infarction with cerebral amyloid angiopathy without distinguishing features.
When sodium levels are explicitly documented (e.g., 129, 125) without dehydration terminology, prefer E86 (Hyponatremia) over E87 (Other fluid and electrolyte disorders).
Hyponatremia documented without explicit mention of dehydration in admission note.
If past medical history includes 'GERD with hiatal hernia', include K21 (Gastroesophageal reflux disease) regardless of admission focus.
GERD with hiatal hernia explicitly mentioned in past medical history.
When past medical history explicitly states 'BPH', include N40 (Benign prostatic hyperplasia) even if unrelated to admission.
BPH documented in past medical history without admission-specific context.
If admission note states initiation of anticoagulants (e.g., 'started on rivaroxaban'), include Z79 (Long term use of anticoagulants).
Anticoagulant initiation documented in admission note without prior history.
When family history includes 'psoriasis', include Z86 (Family history of other diseases) for non-malignant/non-endocrine conditions.
Family history of psoriasis documented without specific code mapping.
If the note describes confusion resolving after medication administration, prefer G92 (Toxic encephalopathy) over F05 (Delirium due to known etiology).
Toxic encephalopathy misclassified as delirium due to known etiology.
When dermatomyositis is explicitly mentioned in the PMH, use M35 (Dermatomyositis) instead of M33 (Polymyositis).
When atraumatic compression fracture is documented with steroid use and PPI, consider M80 (Pathological fracture in osteoporosis) even if imaging details are in discharge notes.
Pathological fracture in osteoporosis missed despite steroid use and atraumatic back pain.
If urinalysis shows leukocytes, nitrites, and bacteria without symptoms, include N39 (Urinary tract infection, unspecified).
Asymptomatic pyuria missed despite positive urinalysis findings.
When confusion follows new tramadol/gabapentin use, add T38 (Tramadol poisoning) and T47 (Gabapentin poisoning) for adverse effects.
Medication-induced confusion missed for tramadol and gabapentin.
Document Z86 (Family history of digestive tract neoplasm) when a sibling has small bowel cancer.
Family history of digestive tract malignancy missed despite brother's small bowel cancer.
Include Z79 (Long-term anticoagulant use) when warfarin is prescribed 5X/WEEK and 2X/WEEK.
Long-term anticoagulant use missed despite warfarin prescriptions.
When 'IPD ectasia with 2 aneurysms' is documented in the admission note, ensure I72 (Aortic aneurysm) is included in the code set.
Failure to code aortic aneurysm despite explicit mention of IPD ectasia with two aneurysms in admission note.
If amLODIPine is listed as a preadmission medication and blood pressure is 136/80, include I10 (Essential hypertension) in the code set.
Omission of hypertension code despite antihypertensive medication and elevated blood pressure in admission note.
When the admission note states 'elective ___ stenting' and 'elective celiac artery and SMA angiogram', include Z23 (Encounter for follow-up care) in the code set.
Missing elective procedure follow-up code despite explicit mention of scheduled stenting in admission note.
If 'cool feet R>L' and 'dopplerable DP pulses b/l' are present in the admission note, include I77 (Atherosclerosis of native arteries of extremities) in the code set.
Failure to code peripheral artery disease despite documentation of cool extremities and Dopplerable pulses in admission note.
When the admission note explicitly states 'pT2N0 stage I rectosigmoid adenocarcinoma' and 'HCC', include Z85 (Personal history of malignant neoplasm) in the code set.
Omission of personal history of malignant neoplasm despite documented rectosigmoid adenocarcinoma and HCC in admission note.
If the admission note documents 'father... treated for colon cancer... prostate cancer', include Z87 (Family history of malignant neoplasm) in the code set.
Missing family history of malignant neoplasm despite documented colon and prostate cancer in paternal family history.
When the admission note states 's/p HBcAb deceased donor liver transplant', include Z90 (Status post organ transplant) in the code set.
Failure to code post-organ transplant status despite explicit documentation of deceased donor liver transplant in admission note.
Avoid including Z94 (Presence of other vascular grafts) unless the admission note explicitly documents vascular prostheses or grafts.
Unjustified inclusion of vascular graft code without documentation of any vascular prosthesis in admission note.
When heart failure (I50) is present alongside hypertension, prefer I11 (hypertension with heart disease) over I10 (essential hypertension).
Hypertension with heart disease (I11) vs. essential hypertension (I10) in a patient with heart failure.
If the note mentions obstructive sleep apnea (OSA) on CPAP, use G47.81 (OSA) instead of G70 (multiple sclerosis).
Sleep apnea (G47) vs. multiple sclerosis (G70) in a patient with OSA on CPAP.
When a preadmission insulin regimen (e.g., glargine) is documented, include Z79 (long-term insulin use) as a comorbidity.
Missed long-term insulin use (Z79) despite explicit mention of glargine insulin in admission note.
Avoid coding I05 (acute rheumatic mitral valve disease) unless the note explicitly mentions rheumatic fever or rheumatic heart disease.
False positive acute rheumatic mitral valve disease (I05) without rheumatic etiology cues.
If the note mentions 'unsteady gait,' prefer R26 over the unspecified R29.
Unsteady gait documented but unspecified abnormal gait code used.
When 'poor nutrition' is documented, assign E43 (Malnutrition) instead of unspecified codes.
Poor nutrition explicitly stated without corresponding malnutrition code.
If a prior fracture is mentioned (e.g., 'right hip fracture s/p arthroplasty'), include Z87 (History of fracture).
History of hip fracture omitted despite explicit documentation.
When a fall is the primary event, include W01 (Accidental fall) as an external cause code.
Fall explicitly described without corresponding external cause code.
If alendronate is listed as a medication, include M81 (Osteoporosis) as a diagnosis.
Osteoporosis medication prescribed without corresponding diagnosis code.
Avoid S06 when CT head explicitly states 'no acute intracranial abnormality.'
Traumatic intracranial injury code assigned despite imaging ruling it out.
Do not assign G93 unless headache is explicitly mentioned in the clinical note.
Headache code used without documented symptoms or findings.
Avoid N39 when UA results are explicitly described as unremarkable.
Urinary tract infection code assigned despite unremarkable UA.
Do not assign E64 unless obesity or BMI is explicitly mentioned in the clinical note.
Obesity code assigned without documented BMI or weight-related findings.
If the note mentions hyperlipidemia (HLD), prefer E78 over E86 for 3-digit code alignment.
Hyperlipidemia (HLD) explicitly mentioned in admission note but coded as E86 instead of E78.
If the note mentions acute pancreatitis with elevated lipase and imaging findings, prefer K85 over K86.
Documentation of acute pancreatitis with elevated lipase and imaging, not chronic.
When ERCP is performed for suspected gallstone pancreatitis with retained stone, include K83 for choledocholithiasis.
ERCP performed for suspected gallstone pancreatitis due to retained stone.
If creatinine levels are elevated in lab results, consider N17 for acute kidney injury rather than N18.
Elevated creatinine levels in lab results suggest acute kidney injury.
When a urinary tract infection is confirmed by culture (e.g., E. coli), include N39 for unspecified UTI.
E. coli urinary tract infection documented in discharge note.
When hypertension and chronic kidney disease are both present, use I13 instead of separate I10 and N18 codes.
Hypertension and chronic kidney disease documented together.
For mild pulmonary edema and hypoxia, prefer J98 for respiratory insufficiency over R09 for nonspecific respiratory symptoms.
Mild pulmonary edema and hypoxia documented in admission note.
When ALT and AST are markedly elevated in lab results, include R91 for abnormal liver function tests.
Elevated liver enzymes (ALT/AST) in lab results.
When warfarin is listed as a preadmission medication, include Z79 for long-term anticoagulant use.
Long-term anticoagulant use (warfarin) documented in admission medications.
For patients with a history of CABG, include Z95 to document the surgical history.
History of coronary artery bypass graft (CABG x3) documented in past medical history.
When family history of CVA is present, include Z86 for personal and family history of cardiovascular disease.
Family history of cerebrovascular accident (CVA) documented in admission note.
When inhaled bronchodilators are prescribed for obstructive symptoms, include J44 for unspecified asthma.
Use of inhaled bronchodilators (Fluticasone-Salmeterol, Ipratropium) suggests obstructive lung disease.
If the note mentions hypertension and heart murmur, prefer I12 over I10.
Hypertension with heart murmur suggests hypertensive heart disease over essential hypertension.
If the note mentions chronic liver disease and EtOH cirrhosis, prefer E83 and E87 over E89.
Chronic liver disease with EtOH cirrhosis requires specific coding over unspecified chronic liver disease.
If the note mentions EtOH cirrhosis, prefer E87 over K70.
Alcoholic liver disease from EtOH cirrhosis should be coded as E87 rather than K70.
If the note mentions liver transplant, prefer Z94 and Z86 over Z91.
History of liver transplant requires specific transplant coding over general organ transplant history.
If the note mentions GERD, prefer K21.8 over K21.
GERD should be coded as K21.8 rather than unspecified K21.
If the note mentions right total knee revision, include Z47.2.
Right total knee revision requires an encounter code for joint replacement revision.
If the note mentions anemia without neoplastic disease, prefer D69 over D64.
Anemia without neoplastic context should be coded as unspecified anemia.
If the note mentions Apixaban for DVT prophylaxis, include Z79.
Long-term anticoagulant use for DVT prophylaxis requires specific coding.
If the note mentions EtOH cirrhosis, include F10.
History of EtOH cirrhosis implies alcohol use disorder coding.
If the note mentions angiosarcoma, use C49.0 (Malignant neoplasm of skin, angiosarcoma) instead of the unspecified C49.
Incorrect code for malignant neoplasm of skin instead of angiosarcoma
If the note mentions radiation dermatitis, use L59 (Radiation dermatitis) instead of L94 (Pressure ulcer).
Incorrect code for pressure ulcer instead of radiation dermatitis
If the note mentions chronic rhinitis without allergic specifics, use R10.9 (Unspecified symptoms involving chest) instead of J30 (Allergic rhinitis).
Incorrect code for allergic rhinitis instead of unspecified chest symptoms
If the note mentions family history of stomach cancer, use Z85.0 (Family history of digestive system cancer) instead of Z80 (Family history of malignant neoplasm).
Incorrect family history code for malignant neoplasm instead of digestive system cancer
If the note mentions scheduled chemotherapy, include Z51.11 (Encounter for antineoplastic chemotherapy) in the codes.
If the note mentions nausea with dry heaves, include R11.0 (Nausea and vomiting) in the codes.
Missing code for nausea and vomiting
If the note mentions anemia due to chemotherapy, include D56 (Anemia in neoplastic disease) in the codes.
Missing code for anemia in neoplastic disease
If the note mentions idiopathic cardiomyopathy with LVEF <50%, prefer I42 over I25 for cardiomyopathy coding.
Mention of ischemic cardiomyopathy without explicit ischemic etiology in admission note.
When LVEF is documented as 41-44%, prefer I50 (heart failure) over I31 (preserved EF heart failure).
Mention of heart failure without explicit preserved ejection fraction cues.
Avoid coding I69 when admission note lacks explicit chronic ischemic heart disease terminology.
Absence of chronic ischemic heart disease documentation in admission note.
Avoid coding I47 when admission note lacks explicit mention of heart block or conduction disorders.
No documentation of heart block in admission note.
When rivaroxaban is listed as a medication, include Z79 for long-term anticoagulant use.
Mention of anticoagulant therapy without corresponding Z79 code.
When AFib, cardiomyopathy, and VT are listed in past medical history, include Z86 for history of heart disease.
Mention of heart disease history without corresponding Z86 code.
When 4+ pitting edema, 7-pound weight gain, and diuretic response are documented, include E87 for fluid/electrolyte disorders.
Mention of fluid overload symptoms without corresponding E87 code.
When psoriatic arthritis is explicitly mentioned in the admission note, prioritize M06 over M09.
Psoriatic arthritis misclassified as unspecified rheumatism.
For chronic productive cough without pneumonia, use R05 instead of R09.
Chronic cough misclassified as unspecified respiratory symptoms.
Peripheral neuropathy without hereditary context should be coded as G62, not G70.
Hereditary neuropathy code used for peripheral neuropathy.
Avoid K76 unless admission note explicitly mentions liver pathology or dysfunction.
Unspecified liver disorder code used without supporting evidence.
Z88 requires explicit mention of communicable disease exposure in admission note.
Exposure to communicable diseases code without clinical justification.
Documented psoriatic skin lesions in admission note warrant L40 coding.
Psoriasis code omitted despite skin lesion documentation.
Sinus bradycardia with RBBB in admission note supports I45 coding.
Bradycardia code omitted despite EKG findings.
Documented bradycardia (HR 44) in admission note requires R00.2, not R09.
If RUQ pain is described with a prior similar episode and imaging shows cholelithiasis, prefer K80 (Biliary colic) over R10 (Abdominal pain) as the specific biliary etiology is implied.
Right upper quadrant pain with prior similar episode without acute cholecystitis documentation.

If the note only lists depression in past medical history without current episode details, avoid F32 (Major depressive episode) and use Z86.79 (Personal history of other mental and behavioral disorder
Mention of depression without explicit depressive episode documentation.
If omeprazole is prescribed for heartburn without ulcer-specific findings, use K21 (Gastric ulcer) only if there is explicit mention of ulceration, not for GERD.
GERD medication use without explicit gastric ulcer documentation.
If the note mentions Truvada without confirming HIV disease, prefer Z21 (HIV positive status) over B20 (HIV disease).
Truvada use without explicit HIV disease documentation.
If the note mentions intoxication at the time of injury, include V13 (Alcohol abuse, current or history) as an external cause.
Intoxication at injury time without alcohol abuse history code.
If the note describes a collision with a parked car, include Y92 (Place of occurrence, other) as an external cause.
Collision with parked car without external cause coding.
If the note mentions confusion after head trauma, include R40 (Confusion) as a cognitive symptom.
Post-traumatic confusion without cognitive symptom coding.
If the note documents FPL or FDP weakness in the hand, include S64 (Injury of wrist and hand) to capture hand trauma.
FPL/FDP weakness without hand injury coding.
If the note mentions dicyclomine prescribed for irritable bowel, include K58 for irritable bowel syndrome.
Mention of dicyclomine use for irritable bowel without explicit IBS documentation.
Avoid including T39/Y92 unless the note explicitly mentions anticonvulsant use or poisoning.
Inclusion of poisoning codes without anticonvulsant exposure or toxicity cues.
Only assign R91 when the note explicitly documents abnormal clinical chemistry findings as a diagnosis.
Abnormal lab findings without specific diagnostic context for R91.
Only assign Z80 if the note explicitly documents a history of tobacco use.
Tobacco use history code without explicit smoking status in admission note.
If the note lists opioid medications for pain management without mentioning poisoning or adverse effects, exclude T40 codes.
Mention of opioid use without evidence of poisoning or adverse effects.
When opioids are listed in admission medications, include K59 for constipation as a secondary diagnosis.
Opioid use documented in admission medications as a risk factor for constipation.
If heparin or anticoagulant therapy is documented in admission notes, include Z76 for long-term use.
Anticoagulant therapy initiation mentioned in admission note.
If the note mentions anemia and chronic disease (e.g., cirrhosis), prefer D61 over D69.
Anemia mentioned without explicit chronic disease context.
If the note mentions UTI signs (e.g., dysuria, abnormal UA), consider N30.
UTI signs without explicit cystitis mention.
If the note mentions sinusitis signs (e.g., fluid in sinuses), consider J01.
If the note mentions prostatitis signs (e.g., prostatic tenderness), consider N41.
If the note mentions alcoholic cirrhosis, prefer K70 over K72.
Alcoholic liver disease mentioned without explicit cirrhosis.
If the note mentions prolonged PTT, consider E83.
Prolonged PTT without explicit coagulation defect mention.
If the note mentions venous stasis, use I87.8 instead of I87.
Venous stasis mentioned instead of varicose veins.
When 'alcoholic cirrhosis c/b hepatic encephalopathy' is documented in the admission note, code K70 (Alcoholic liver disease with alcoholic hepatitis) instead of unrelated codes like I10.
Alcoholic cirrhosis with complications explicitly stated in admission history.
If nonbloody emesis, watery stools, and no infectious etiology are described in the admission note, code A08 (Gastroenteritis and colitis, unspecified) as the primary diagnosis.
Gastrointestinal symptoms consistent with viral gastroenteritis in admission note.

When hyponatremia (Na <135) is present in admission labs and diuretics are listed as preadmission medications, code E87 (Disorders of fluid, electrolyte and osmotic balance) and E86 (Disorders of sodi
Hyponatremia documented in admission labs (Na-127) with diuretic use.
Avoid coding I10 (Essential hypertension) when no blood pressure abnormalities or hypertension history are documented in the admission note.
Hypertension code selected despite no evidence in admission note.
Avoid coding E11 (Type 1 diabetes mellitus) when no hyperglycemia, diabetic complications, or diabetes history are documented in the admission note.
Diabetes code selected despite no evidence in admission note.
When NSTEMI is documented with troponin elevation and EKG changes, prefer I21 over I20 for acute myocardial infarction.
NSTEMI with troponin elevation and EKG changes present, but angina code selected instead.
If bacteremia is documented with positive cultures but later deemed contamination, avoid A41 unless clinical progression to sepsis is explicitly stated.
Bacteremia with positive blood cultures documented, but sepsis code included despite discharge note clarification.
When both hypertension and ESRD are documented, prefer I13 over I10 for hypertensive chronic kidney disease.
Hypertension and ESRD documented, but unspecified hypertension code selected instead of hypertensive CKD.
If a mechanical fall is documented as the admission reason, include W01 as an external cause code.
Mechanical fall explicitly mentioned in admission note but no external cause code included.
When a specific fever temperature is documented, include R50 for fever as a separate diagnosis.
Fever documented at 101.7°F but no R50 code included.
If BPH medications like finasteride and tamsulosin are documented, include N40 for benign prostatic hyperplasia.
BPH medications listed but no N40 code for benign prostatic hyperplasia.
When panic disorder is explicitly documented with anxiolytic medication use, include F41 for panic disorder.
Panic disorder with alprazolam use documented but no F41 code included.
If acute blood loss anemia is explicitly documented in the context of a hip fracture, include D62 for anemia due to blood loss.
Acute blood loss anemia documented but no D62 code included.
When ESRD with hemodialysis is documented, prefer Z95 over Z99 to indicate dialysis dependence.
ESRD on hemodialysis documented but Z99 selected instead of Z95 for dialysis dependence.
If pulmonary hypertension is documented on CXR, include R09 for respiratory symptoms.
CXR findings of pulmonary hypertension documented but no R09 code included.
If anemia is documented without marrow failure cues (e.g., pancytopenia, reticulocytopenia), prefer D68 (Anemia in chronic disease) over D62.
Aplastic anemia (D62) selected despite no clinical evidence of bone marrow failure.
When labs show acute creatinine elevation (e.g., 1.6-1.9) without DKA or shock, consider N17 (Acute renal failure) even if discharge note clarifies resolution.
Unspecified renal failure (N17) omitted despite creatinine elevation in admission labs.
For SOB with hypoxia (92% O2 sat), prefer J96 (Respiratory insufficiency due to heart failure) over J98 if heart failure is documented.
Unspecified respiratory insufficiency (J98) included without chronic disease context.
When 'mechanical AVR' is documented in medications, ensure Z95 is included to capture the prosthetic device status.
Prosthetic cardiac device (Z95) omitted despite explicit mention of mechanical AVR in admission meds.
If aortic stenosis is mentioned with worsening gradients, include I35 for aortic stenosis.
Aortic stenosis documented in admission note without explicit post-procedure mismatch mention.
If low platelets are documented without other causes identified, consider D69 for idiopathic thrombocytopenic purpura.
Low platelets mentioned in admission note without explicit ITP diagnosis.
If CHF exacerbation and home oxygen are mentioned, include I50 for heart failure.
CHF exacerbation and home oxygen use documented in admission note.
If CABG is mentioned in past surgical history, include Z86 for history of CABG.
CABG history explicitly listed in admission note.
If HTN is mentioned alongside heart disease, prefer I12 over I10 for hypertensive heart disease.
